label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out why foreign kids want to learn Chinese based on the given passage. Let me read through the text again to make sure I understand the key points. The passage starts by mentioning that while English is important in schools, Chinese is popular among foreign kids. It gives an example of a 16-year-old from South Korea who wants to study at Peking University, so he's learning Chinese even though he finds it difficult. The article then talks about the number of Chinese learners in South Korea and other countries, mentioning that nearly 130 million people in 85 countries are learning Chinese, expecting this to reach 800 million. It also notes that in the US, Chinese is the second most popular foreign language after Spanish. The reasons given for people wanting to learn Chinese are because China is becoming an important country, which helps with business understanding, and the Chinese government is promoting it by sending teachers and setting up Confucius Colleges. Now, looking at the question: Why do foreign kids want to learn Chinese? The options are: A) Because Chinese is getting more important in their schools. B) Because Chinese characters have too many strokes. C) Because the number of people learning Chinese will be 800 million. D) Because China is becoming more and more important in the world. Looking back at the text, the passage states that "People want to learn Chinese because China is becoming such an important country. Foreign countries want to understand China better to help them with business." So this directly points to option D. Option A seems to be a distractor; the first sentence mentions English getting more important in schools, but the question is about why they want to learn Chinese. The passage does mention that Chinese is popular among foreign kids, but the reason given is China's importance, not the school's emphasis. Option B is actually presented as a challenge by the South Korean student Piao, who finds Chinese difficult because of the many strokes, but this is not the reason he or others are learning it. The student still wants to learn Chinese despite the difficulty, so B is a negative point, not a reason for learning. Option C refers to
